Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Evidence And Monetary Sanctions; Motion To Compel Responses And Deem Requests For Admissions, Admitted
Off Calendar Dec-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Haiqing Qiu, 415-568-6784, appearing on his own behalf as pro per plaintiff and is assisted by Winnie Chan, Provisional Interperter for Cantonese;
Adam M. Stoddard, Esq., Sheuerman, Martini, Tabari et al, 408-286-9700, appearing for Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital;

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Evidence And Monetary Sanctions; Motion To Compel Responses And Deem Requests For Admissions, Admitted

The motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. "[C]ourts have long recognized that the terminating sanction is a drastic penalty and should be used sparingly." J.W. v. Watchtower Bible and Tract Socy. of New York, Inc. (2018) 29 Cal. App. 5th 1142, 1169. "The trial court should select a sanction that is 'tailor[ed] ... to the harm caused by the withheld discovery'" and does "not exceed that which is required to protect the interests of the party entitled to but denied discovery." Ibid. (internal citations omitted). Here, Plaintiff failed to comply with a clear order to appear for deposition. (Oct. 10, 2019, Order of Judge Pro Tem Gallo). CCP 2023.010(g). Plaintiff failed to respond to discovery requests seeking, among other things, evidence on which Plaintiff's successful opposition to the demurrer was based. CCP 2023.010(d). Additionally, Plaintiff failed to respond to requests for admissions, and Defendant's motion to deem admitted must be granted. CCP 2033.280. Deeming the requests for admissions admitted would for all practical purposes preclude introduction of evidence to support Plaintiff's claims. Rather than requiring a two-step process by which Defendant moves for summary judgment on the deemed admitted RFAs, I find that an order dismissing the action of Plaintiff against Dignity Community Care dba St. Francis Memorial Hospital is appropriate under CCP 2023.030(d). The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $4,015.80. CCP 2033.280(c) (sanctions are mandatory).

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL's Rescheduled Hearing On Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint.

Defendant Dignity Community Care dba Saint Francis Memorial Hospital's motion to strike the claim for attorney's fees in Plaintiff's complaint is granted. No opposition filed. Alperson (1975) 25 Cal.3d 124, 127 ["Unless authorized by either statute or agreement, attorney's fees ordinarily are not recoverable as costs."].) Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Haiging Qui, 415-5686784 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Mandarin Court Interpreter Aidong Ni assists Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL's Rescheduled Hearing On Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint.

Defendant Dignity Community Care dba Saint Francis Memorial Hospital's demurrer to Plaintiff Haiqing Qiu's complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's sole cause of action for medical malpractice is governed by the one-year limitations provision of Code of Civil Procedure section 340.5. The complaint and Plaintiff's opposition indicate that the alleged malpractice occurred on October 18, 2017, when Plaintiff alleges he witnessed medical staff refusing to treat his mother and prematurely declaring her deceased. The complaint was not filed until January 16, 2019, more than one year after Plaintiff discovered the alleged malpractice, and therefore appears on its face to be barred by the statute of limitations.

The Court prepares and signs its order. (see scanned order for details)
